data.title: In HelloNation, Tax Expert Dr. LaTonya Perryman Explains How Audit Protection Helps Taxpayers
data.description:
In HelloNation, Tax Expert Dr. LaTonya Perryman Explains How Audit Protection Helps Taxpayers

In HelloNation, Tax Expert Dr. LaTonya Perryman Explains How Audit Protection Helps Taxpayers

PR Newswire

CLARKSDALE, Miss., July 1, 2026

The article highlights how preparation, documentation, and professional guidance can help taxpayers respond confidently to IRS inquiries.

CLARKSDALE, Miss., July 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- What should taxpayers do when they receive a notice from the IRS? HelloNation answers that question in an article featuring insights from Dr. LaTonya Perryman of Divine Dynasty Solutions in Clarksdale, MS.

The article explains that receiving an IRS notice can be stressful, even for taxpayers who believe their tax return was prepared and filed correctly. While an IRS notice does not automatically indicate a serious problem, understanding available support options can help taxpayers feel more informed and prepared if questions arise.

According to the article, audit protection services are designed to assist taxpayers when concerns or inquiries involve a tax return. Although audit protection services cannot prevent an audit or guarantee a specific outcome, they can provide guidance, documentation support, and assistance with responding to requests from tax authorities. For many taxpayers, access to audit support can make an unfamiliar process feel more manageable.

One common misconception discussed in the article is that audits only affect individuals who have made mistakes. In reality, a tax return may be selected for review for many reasons, including reporting discrepancies, deductions, business expenses, or even random selection. In many situations, taxpayers simply need to provide supporting documentation that verifies information already reported.

The article emphasizes the importance of accurate recordkeeping throughout the year. Maintaining organized tax records can make it easier to respond when questions arise. Receipts, income statements, bank records, charitable contribution records, and business expense information may all become important forms of supporting documentation during a review.

Audit protection services often focus on helping taxpayers gather and organize the materials needed to address requests from tax authorities. Rather than trying to determine independently which documents are required, taxpayers may receive documentation support that helps identify the most relevant records. This type of documentation support can reduce confusion and improve efficiency during the response process.

Another key benefit involves understanding official correspondence. The article notes that an IRS notice may contain deadlines, requests for information, or explanations that are difficult for some taxpayers to interpret. Audit support often includes guidance that helps taxpayers understand what the notice means and what steps should be taken next.

Timely action is also important. The article explains that delaying a response to an IRS notice can sometimes create additional complications. Audit protection services may help taxpayers understand response timelines and organize supporting documentation before deadlines expire. This assistance can be particularly valuable when complex financial records are involved.

Business owners may find audit support especially useful because business tax returns often include additional reporting requirements. Expenses, payroll records, deductions, and equipment purchases can generate questions that require detailed explanations. Maintaining tax records and practicing accurate recordkeeping can help simplify the process if documentation is requested.

Preparation is another central theme of the article. Effective audit protection services often emphasize proactive organization long before an IRS notice arrives. Through accurate recordkeeping and properly maintained tax records, taxpayers can position themselves to respond more effectively if questions arise later.

The article also notes that many taxpayers mistakenly assume an IRS notice automatically means they are facing a full audit. In reality, many notices involve limited requests for clarification or additional supporting documentation. Understanding the nature of the inquiry and having access to audit support can help taxpayers avoid unnecessary stress.

The article concludes that audit protection services are designed to help taxpayers navigate IRS inquiries with greater confidence. By providing documentation support, encouraging accurate recordkeeping, organizing tax records, and assisting with supporting documentation, these services can help individuals and business owners respond more effectively when questions arise regarding a tax return.

How Audit Protection Helps Taxpayers features insights from Dr. LaTonya Perryman, Tax Expert of Clarksdale, MS, in HelloNation.

About HelloNation

HelloNation is America's Good News Network, a premier media platform built on the idea that good news travels faster when real people tell real stories. Through its community-focused publications and innovative "edvertising" approach, HelloNation delivers content that informs, inspires, and spotlights the leaders making a meaningful impact in their communities.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/in-hellonation-tax-expert-dr-latonya-perryman-explains-how-audit-protection-helps-taxpayers-302815983.html

SOURCE HelloNation

data.title: Marcus Theatres Records Highest June Revenue of All Time
data.description:
Marcus Theatres Records Highest June Revenue of All Time

Tremendous success of Toy Story 5 and strong performances from multiple exciting films drove June records at Marcus Theatres

Marcus Theatres®, the nation’s fourth largest theatre circuit and a division of Marcus Corporation (NYSE: MCS), announced today that it recorded the highest total revenue for June in company history.

These record results were driven by sustained excitement among Marcus Theatres’ moviegoers for the record-breaking debut of Toy Story 5, a well-rounded slate of June releases including Scary Movie and Disclosure Day, and continued strong carryover performances from films like Obsession and Backrooms. Records broken during June include:

  • The highest total revenue ever in the month of June.
  • Highest concession, merchandise, and food and beverage revenue ever in the month of June.
  • Record admission revenue per person for the month of June.
  • Record concession, merchandise, and food and beverage revenue per person for the month of June.
  • The highest box office since 2019 for the month of June.
  • Record single-day attendance for the Marcus Mystery Movie program with 18% of total attendance coming from the mystery title on Monday, June 29. Marcus Mystery Movie is an opportunity for moviegoers to catch a screening of an upcoming movie before its official release date with the title revealed at showtime.

More significantly, based on the domestic box office, we estimate that over 90 million people attended the movies in June. This substantial percentage of the United States population demonstrates their motivation to invest their valuable time and effort in Hollywood’s product, highlighting the immense power and wide-reaching influence of theatrical exhibition.

“Throughout June, moviegoers showed up in force to see an incredible lineup of great movies on the big screen,” said Jeff Tomachek, president of Marcus Theatres. “Without a doubt, Toy Story 5 delivered in record breaking fashion. But we also had unexpected blockbusters in Backrooms and Obsession that are still drawing moviegoers to the theatre. All this after strong and sometimes record-breaking turn out earlier in the year for movies like Project Hail Mary, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, Michael and The Devil Wears Prada 2. Congratulations to our associates for helping drive these results by making magical movie memories for every guest who walks in our door.”

The remainder of the summer film slate features more exciting and highly anticipated new films at Marcus Theatres, including Minions & Monsters (July 1), Moana (July 10), The Odyssey (July 17), Spider-Man: Brand New Day (July 31), Super Troopers 3 (August 7), Paw Patrol: The Dino Movie (August 14), Insidious: Out of the Further (August 21), and Coyote vs. Acme (August 28).

Looking ahead to the rest of the year, additional films that are expected to perform well include: Practical Magic 2 (September 11), Resident Evil (September 18), Forgotten Island (September 25), Digger (October 2), Verity (October 2), Other Mommy (October 9), The Social Reckoning (October 9), Street Fighter (October 16), The Cat in the Hat (November 6), Godzilla Minus Zero (November 6), Hunger Games: Sunrise on the Reaping (November 20), Hexed (November 25), Focker-In-Law (November 25), Dune: Part Three (December 18), Avengers: Doomsday (December 18), The Angry Birds Movie 3 (December 23)and Jumanji: Open World (December 25).

Tickets and showtimes for all Marcus Theatres and Movie Tavern locations can be found at marcustheatres.com and the Marcus Theatres mobile app.

About Marcus Theatres

Marcus Theatres®, a division of Marcus Corporation, is the fourth-largest theatre circuit in the United States and currently owns or operates 975 screens at 77 locations in 17 states under the Marcus Theatres, Movie Tavern® by Marcus and BistroPlex® brands. For more information, please visit www.marcustheatres.com and follow the company on Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok.

About Marcus Corporation

Headquartered in Milwaukee, Marcus Corporation is a leader in the entertainment and hospitality industries, with significant company-owned real estate assets. In addition to its Marcus Theatres division, its hospitality division, Marcus® Hotels & Resorts, owns and/or manages 17 hotels, resorts and other properties in eight states. For more information, please visit the company’s website at www.marcuscorp.com.

For more information contact:
AngelaSmetana@marcustheatres.com

data.title: Eurozone's Risks to Inflation, Growth Are Now More Balanced, Lagarde Says
data.description:
By Ed Frankl and Don Forbes 




  Risks to the eurozone's inflation and economic growth are more balanced after the decline in energy prices in the last month, European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said Wednesday. 



  "Risks...that we had to the upside on inflation and to the downside of growth are probably more broadly balanced than they were a few weeks ago as a result of what we are seeing," she said on a panel at the ECB's forum in Sintra, Portugal. 



  Oil prices declined to $72 a barrel in June from $120 a barrel in March, Lagarde noted. Price changes occurred at such a pace that central bankers should become prepared to measure them using different tools such as artificial intelligence, she added. 



  Inflation in the eurozone fell in June by more than expected, driven not just by lower fuel costs, but by cooling services prices, suggesting the recent energy-price surge hasn't broadened out cost pressures significantly to other areas of the economy. 



  The inflation data confirms the ECB will hold its key interest rate this month, economists say, but investors still expect at least one rate hike by the end of the year. 





  Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com and Don Forbes at don.forbes@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  July 01, 2026 10:40 ET (14:40 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Your aging parent has a new romance. Here's how to protect your inheritance - without looking greedy.
data.description:
By Morey Stettner

Before your single mom or dad remarries, talk with them about estate-planning issues

Adult children watching their single mom or dad in a new, serious relationship often have mixed emotions when it comes to money.

As more people live longer, many lose a spouse. But rather than remain a widow or widower, they remarry. Finding fresh love at an advanced age brings joy to one's golden years. But the retiree's adult children may not share that sentiment.

Let's be blunt: If you have a single mom or dad in their 70s or older who's in a new, serious relationship, you may worry about your inheritance.

If your parent remarries - and then dies - will the surviving spouse wind up with a big chunk of the estate that would've gone to you?

"A lot of parents in new relationships end up, unbeknownst to them, disinheriting their children," said Stacy Francis, a New York City-based certified financial planner. "They leave all their money to their new spouse and trust that after they die, the new spouse will leave it equally to all the [deceased spouse's] biological children. That typically doesn't happen."

Instead, the surviving spouse can redeploy the funds at will. This leaves disgruntled adult children weighing what to do to reclaim money they feel is rightfully theirs.

Ideally, an aging parent will enlist an estate-planning attorney to set up a marital trust and appoint a reliable trustee to carry out their wishes. This protects them and their children. Estate-planning laws vary by state. A lack of a legally binding trust, will and other documents can expose an estate to adverse outcomes and high taxes.

A parent might also draft a prenuptial agreement for their new spouse to sign. Aside from stipulating how to divide assets, it safeguards which children from prior relationships will receive an inheritance.

Once your parent agrees to hire an estate-planning attorney, confirm that mom or dad retains the lawyer exclusively, without their new partner. Attorneys have a fiduciary duty to do what's best for their client.

"Typically, whoever signs the client agreement is the person to whom the attorney has a fiduciary duty," Francis said. "If a couple hires the attorney together, the attorney has a duty to both."

Urge your parent to meet privately with the attorney before inviting the new spouse to attend. This is especially important if your parent has significantly more assets.

"The [richer] spouse may be comfortable meeting with the attorney on their own," Francis said. "Then they can have a second meeting with the new spouse in the room so that there are no surprises."

Adult children watching their single mom or dad fall madly in love can experience a mix of emotions. You can be happy for your parent while worrying that your inheritance is at risk.

The tricky part is guiding your parent to make sound decisions without coming across as greedy. You don't want to alienate your mom or dad, or make them feel that you don't support their late-in-life shot at romantic bliss.

"It's an extremely sensitive conversation," said Alex Dann, a certified financial planner in Charlotte, N.C. "There are a million directions it can go, positively or negatively. It's hard not to look like you're being selfish."

Both Francis and Dann suggested that the best way to raise the topic is starting from a place of love. You might say: "Dad, you know how much I love you and want you to be happy. Now might be a good time to meet with an estate-planning attorney to make sure everyone understands your wishes and honors them."

Perhaps the hardest thing for adult children to accept: They're not entitled to an inheritance. As long as their parent is cognitively sharp and not enmeshed in a romance scam, the size and scope of an inheritance is entirely up to the parent. The estate may not be divided equally among the children - or in ways they deem fair.

"Some children might say that the new spouse has nefarious intent," said Jim Crider, a certified financial planner in New Braunfels, Texas. "If you say that, you risk severing your relationship with your parent."

Still, there are steps you can take to prevent financial loss.

Review your parent's personal umbrella insurance policy to ensure the liability limit covers their net worth. And confirm they named a trustworthy executor of their estate, completed all legal documents (such as power of attorney for medical and financial decisions) and enrolled in bill-pay services to simplify their household bookkeeping.

"You want to set up checks and balances," Francis said. "That helps to prevent fraud."

Even then, brace for the unexpected.

"Adult kids often believe they'll get larger inheritances than the reality," she added. "Parents are living longer, getting remarried and needing more medical care" that can drain their assets.

-Morey Stettner

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  07-01-26 1040ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: TCC Expands Retail Footprint Through Acquisition of More Than 30 Stores
data.description:
TCC Expands Retail Footprint Through Acquisition of More Than 30 Stores

TCC Expands Retail Footprint Through Acquisition of More Than 30 Stores

PR Newswire

FISHERS, Ind., July 1, 2026
  • Verizon Authorized Retailer Strengthens Northeast Presence Through Strategic Acquisition
  • Deal Reinforces Company's Long-Term Growth and Commitment to Exceptional Customer Experiences

FISHERS, Ind., July 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- TCC, a Verizon authorized retailer and a subsidiary of Round Room, LLC, announces the acquisition of 36 retail locations from R Wireless, further expanding its national footprint and reinforcing the company's long-term growth strategy.

Effective July 1, the acquisition strengthens TCC's presence throughout the Northeast, expanding its retail footprint and creating new opportunities to serve customers across the region. The newly acquired stores span across five states including: Connecticut, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania. In addition to the new stores, TCC will welcome over 100 R Wireless employees into their family.

"This is a milestone acquisition for TCC and another major step in our long-term growth strategy," said Scott Moorehead, CEO of Round Room. "Beyond expanding our footprint, what excites us most is the opportunity to welcome these new communities to TCC and share who we are as a company, including our commitment to giving back and making a positive impact where we live and work."

To ensure a seamless transition, more than 100 members of TCC's sales team will support the newly acquired locations. They will be joined by a significant number of Store Support team members who will provide hands-on assistance with infrastructure, operations and onboarding. The strategic investment is further amplified by the strong and sustainable Verizon infrastructure that already exists in the area, enabling TCC to provide more opportunities for customers to bring their mobile and home services together.

"The future of TCC has never been brighter," said Moorehead. "As we grow our network, we're committed to investing in our people, enhancing our operations, and delivering the exceptional service our customers have come to expect. This acquisition positions us for continued success, and we're excited about what comes next."

With this company milestone, TCC's store count reaches 594, putting Round Room's total store count at 1,402. The acquisition also builds on a recent key appointment, the promotion of Jeff Falk to Area Vice President for the Northeast region. Previously serving as a regional director, Falk lives in the area and is actively involved in his community, making him well positioned to support TCC's teams, customers, and overall continued growth.

TCC operates under parent company Round Room, LLC, a holding company of emerging technologies and businesses in the wireless space. The Verizon wireless retailer operates nearly 600 locations across the U.S., employing more than 2,000 people and serving thousands of customers every day.

Supported by over 400 communities, TCC actively invests in local and national causes. Community giveback initiatives are a core part of Round Room and each of its entities' DNA as the company regularly organizes events that leave a positive impact on the communities it serves. On a larger, national scale, they host quarterly initiatives that support teachers, pet rescues and those affected by domestic violence. In addition to these collective events, Round Room has donated more than $11M through its community grant program where stores have supported over 2,000 nonprofit organizations, hospitals, and more. This culture of giving also strengthens the company, earning TCC national recognition for their positive work environment and industry-leading employee satisfaction.

To learn more about TCC, visit www.TCCRocks.com.

About Round Room, LLC
Founded on a mission and deep commitment of giving back to employees, customers, and the communities it serves, Round Room is one of the largest Verizon-authorized retailers in the U.S. Its collective portfolio of brands includes TCC, Wireless Zone, and Culture of Good. With more than 1,400 TCC and Wireless Zone retail locations across 43 states, Round Room has donated more than $11M to various causes through ongoing giveback initiatives. The company's efforts have also been recognized through the Top Workplaces USA 2025 award and Top Workplaces Culture Excellence award. To learn more about Round Room, visit www.RoundRoom.com.

About TCC
Founded in 1991, TCC is a Verizon wireless retailer that operates nearly 600 locations from coast to coast and employs upwards of 2,200 people. As a certified 'Culture of Good' company, TCC encourages employees to give back in every community it serves through its Community Grant program, volunteer opportunities, and local philanthropic events. TCC has been recognized with several notable awards including Inc. Magazine's Best in Business, Top Workplaces USA, and Glassdoor's Best Places to Work. To learn more about TCC or to find a location near you, visit www.TCCRocks.com. For more information about TCC's parent company, Round Room, LLC, visit www.RoundRoom.com.

MEDIA CONTACT: Ethan Gutstein, Fishman Public Relations, egutstein@fishmanpr.com or (847) 945-1300

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/tcc-expands-retail-footprint-through-acquisition-of-more-than-30-stores-302815954.html

SOURCE TCC

data.title: Space Stocks Get a Lift from NASA Contracts
data.description:
By Connor Hart 




  Shares of Firefly Aerospace, Intuitive Machines and Voyager Technologies rose after NASA awarded new contracts to deliver equipment to the Moon, as the agency works to build a lunar outpost. 



  Firefly Aerospace's stock gained 1.1% on Wednesday to $29.73. Shares of Intuitive Machines ticked up 0.4% to $21.48, while shares of Voyager Technologies climbed 9.9% to $35.43. 



  Firefly Aerospace, Intuitive Machines and Astrobotic late Tuesday won awards from the National Aeronautics and Space Administration to ferry payloads to the lunar surface by the end of 2028. 



  Astrobotic, which has agreed to be taken over by Voyager Technologies, won two flights--valued at roughly $298 million--carrying the NASA payloads to the moon. Firefly and Intuitive secured one mission each, valued at $144 million and $148 million, respectively. 





  Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  July 01, 2026 10:35 ET (14:35 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Leading Texas Firm Rebrands as Carter Arnett Stahl + Cho Hernandez, Elevating Two Elite Litigators as Name Partners
data.description:
Leading Texas Firm Rebrands as Carter Arnett Stahl + Cho Hernandez, Elevating Two Elite Litigators as Name Partners

Name change signals a new era of diverse leadership and legal advocacy

The Dallas-based litigation and trial law firm Carter Arnett PLLC is pleased to announce it has changed its name to Carter Arnett Stahl + Cho Hernandez PLLC, adding the names of partners Linda Stahl and Stacey Cho Hernandez.

The name change recognizes the immense contributions and legal excellence of the new name partners, who join firm co-founders and principals E. Leon Carter and J. Robert Arnett as the firm solidifies its reputation as a trial powerhouse for complex, high-stakes litigation.

"Being named a name partner alongside Leon, Bob and Stacey is an incredible honor," said Ms. Stahl. "We have built a culture that values meticulous preparation and fierce, ethical advocacy. This new chapter allows us to continue expanding our footprint in high-stakes commercial and civil disputes while maintaining the standard of excellence our clients expect from us."

"Linda and Stacey represent the absolute best of the legal profession, and their elevation to name partners is a natural reflection of the leadership they’ve provided for years," said Mr. Carter. "As Carter Arnett Stahl + Cho Hernandez, we want to be known as a high-stakes and higher-standards law firm. Our clients come to us when the stakes are at their absolute greatest level. They stay with us because our rigorous standards deliver results.”

Carter Arnett was founded in Dallas with a mission of combining the courtroom depth and sophisticated capabilities of a national firm with the hyper-focused attention and agility unique to a trial boutique firm.

The transition marks a defining moment for the firm as it positions itself for a new era under a name that reflects its diverse leadership and top-tier capabilities.

"I am thrilled to step into this role as we continue to take on complex, business-critical matters," said Ms. Cho Hernandez. “Our team thrives under pressure, and we are ready to show the legal community what a firm dedicated to higher standards can achieve."

As Carter Arnett Stahl + Cho Hernandez, the firm will continue to counsel Fortune 500 companies, public entities, entrepreneurs and individuals across an array of practices, including commercial disputes, employment law, intellectual property and appellate litigation.

About Carter Arnett Stahl + Cho Hernandez PLLC

Based in Dallas, Texas, Carter Arnett Stahl + Cho Hernandez PLLC is a top-tier litigation firm representing domestic and international clients in state and federal courts. Renowned for combining big-firm quality with specialized attention and trial readiness, the firm handles diverse high-stakes disputes, from intellectual property and antitrust to commercial litigation and employment. Backed by an experienced team of former judicial clerks and proven trial attorneys, Carter Arnett Stahl + Cho Hernandez PLLC is consistently ranked among the nation's elite legal teams.

Media Contact:
Fabian Gutierrez
fabian@androvett.com
800-559-4534

data.title: As Global Markets Boom and Roar to Record and All-Time Highs, ELEKTROS Strengthens Its Vision for High-Speed EV Charging Infrastructure
data.description:
Company focus expands toward high-speed EV charging stations as global electric vehicle adoption continues to highlight the need for broader, faster and more reliable charging infrastructure

WEST PALM BEACH, FL / ACCESS Newswire / July 1, 2026 / ELEKTROS Inc. (OTC Pink:ELEK) announced that, following correspondence regarding U.S. Patent No. 12,522,100 B1, the Company has reviewed Jaguar Land Rover's response and has elected not to pursue the matter further. Management believes this allows the Company to focus its attention on executing its strategic growth initiatives.

The Company is negotiating to secure a location for approximately 10 to 15 high-speed EV charging stations to operate under the ELEKTROS brand. In parallel, the Company is in discussions with a major U.S. EV charging infrastructure installer regarding a potential installation project, subject to completing definitive agreements.

Management believes the proposed charging-station initiative, if completed, could represent a meaningful step toward building a physical ELEKTROS presence in the EV infrastructure market. The Company intends to evaluate charging-site economics, installation requirements, operating logistics, branding opportunities and potential customer demand as it continues discussions with potential infrastructure participants.

"This is an exciting step for ELEKTROS as we focus on building our business and executing our long-term vision," said Shlomo Bleier, Chief Executive Officer of ELEKTROS Inc. "As EV adoption continues to move forward, we believe charging access, speed and reliability remain central issues for consumers, automakers and infrastructure operators. ELEKTROS is focused on exploring a practical path toward participating in that future."

Industry Background: Verified Public Reporting on EV Charging Infrastructure

  • Elon Musk / Reuters: Reuters reported on May 10, 2024 that Tesla CEO Elon Musk said Tesla would spend more than $500 million in 2024 to expand its fast-charging network. Musk stated: "Tesla will spend well over $500M expanding our Supercharger network to create thousands of NEW chargers this year."

  • Reuters: Reuters reported that the planned Supercharger expansion followed Tesla workforce reductions and still reflected a stated commitment to expand fast-charging infrastructure.

  • Benzinga: Benzinga reported that Tesla opened select U.S. Supercharger stations to non-Tesla EVs and quoted Tesla as stating: "Access to an extensive, convenient and reliable fast-charging network is critical for large-scale EV adoption."

  • The Wall Street Journal: The Wall Street Journal reported in October 2025 that U.S. fast-charging ports increased by more than 80% over two years, reaching more than 60,300 by August, citing federal data from the Joint Office of Energy and Transportation.

  • Additional market context: Recent public reporting has continued to identify charging availability and access to fast charging as major considerations for EV drivers and fleet operators.

Strategic Focus

ELEKTROS believes the growth of electric vehicles globally may create opportunities for companies focused on high-speed charging locations, installation execution, site branding and infrastructure support. The Company's current focus remains on negotiating potential charging locations and evaluating a possible installation project, while maintaining disciplined attention to definitive agreements, customary conditions and practical execution.

Forward-Looking Statements:

This news release contains forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties. Actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied. Negotiations and proposed projects remain subject to execution of definitive agreements and customary conditions. Statements regarding potential charging-station locations, infrastructure installation, branding, market opportunities, operating plans and future growth initiatives are forward-looking and are not guarantees of future performance.

Contact Information

ELEKTROS Inc.

West Palm Beach, Florida
Phone: 786-477-9003
Email: elektrosinc@gmail.com
Website: https://elektros.energy

Source Notes for Industry Background

  • Reuters, May 10, 2024, reporting Elon Musk statement on Tesla Supercharger expansion. https://www.reuters.com/technology/elon-musk-says-tesla-will-spend-500-mln-expand-charging-network-2024-05-10/

  • Elon Musk post on X, May 10, 2024, regarding Tesla Supercharger expansion. https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1788834859110002716

  • Benzinga, March 1, 2023, reporting Tesla Supercharger access and large-scale EV adoption statement. https://www.benzinga.com/news/23/03/31128427/tesla-supercharger-stations-now-open-for-rivals-in-the-united-states-heres-how-it-works-and-why-its

  • The Wall Street Journal, Oct. 3, 2025, reporting growth in U.S. fast-charging ports. https://www.wsj.com/business/autos/america-keeps-adding-ev-chargers-will-there-be-more-drivers-to-use-them-dd247fa6

SOURCE: Elektros, Inc.
